daily_brief: The day's news is dominated by escalating US-Iran tensions following the burial of Supreme Leader Khamenei in Mashhad, with renewed strikes threatening to derail the fragile ceasefire [BBC], [Globe and Mail]. Israeli intelligence reportedly shared details of a 'specific' Iranian plot to assassinate President Trump, potentially explaining his unusual plane switch after the NATO summit [France 24], [The Hindu]. In Europe, at least 11 people died in a devastating wildfire in southern Spain's Almería province, with four victims believed to be British nationals trapped in their vehicle as temperatures exceeded 40°C [BBC Europe], [Al Jazeera]. France's climate watchdog warned the country is unprepared for accelerating climate impacts after recording 2.2°C of warming since 1900 [RFI]. On a lighter note, the Bayeux Tapestry completed a high-security overnight journey to London's British Museum—its first return to English soil in nearly 1,000 years [BBC Europe], [France 24]. In technology and markets, South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion in the largest-ever US listing by a foreign company [BBC], [Financial Times], while China achieved its first successful reusable rocket booster recovery [BBC], [The Hindu].

top_stories.headline: US-Iran Strikes Resume as Khamenei Buried, Threatening Fragile Ceasefire
bullets: Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was buried at the Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad following six days of funeral processions across Iran and Iraq
The US launched air strikes against Iran on Thursday, with Tehran responding by targeting Bahrain, Kuwait, and Qatar; Iran accused the US of striking near its sole nuclear power plant
Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed the US bombed two bridges on the Tehran-Mashhad railway line overnight
Mojtaba Khamenei, who succeeded his father as supreme leader, has not appeared publicly since reportedly being seriously wounded in the February 28 strike that killed his father

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on funeral details and succession uncertainty around Mojtaba Khamenei

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized ceasefire collapse and 'Kill Trump' placards at burial ceremony

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Provided live coverage format with regional security implications

top_stories.headline: Israeli Intelligence Reveals 'Specific' Iranian Plot to Assassinate Trump
bullets: Israel shared intelligence with the US this week about a new and 'specific' Iranian plan to assassinate President Trump, according to CNN and Wall Street Journal reports citing unnamed sources
The intelligence may explain Trump's unusual decision to switch planes when departing the NATO summit in Turkey, sending his new Qatari-gifted jet ahead to Britain
Tehran has vowed retaliation against Trump since he ordered the assassination of Iranian General Qassem Soleimani in January 2020
Trump confirmed awareness of threats, stating 'I'm on every single one of their lists'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Led with Israeli intelligence angle and connected to NATO summit logistics

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Placed story within broader context of West Asia war updates

sources.name: CNN (cited)
sources.framing_note: Original reporting source on 'steady drumbeat' of assassination intelligence

top_stories.headline: Deadly Wildfire Kills At Least 11 in Southern Spain Amid Extreme Heatwave
bullets: At least 11 people died and 19 remain missing in a wildfire near Los Gallardos in Almería province, Andalusia
Four victims, believed to be British nationals, were found trapped in a right-hand-drive vehicle; seven others were found elsewhere apparently trying to flee
Regional officials said the fire was likely caused by a downed power line and spread rapidly through wooded areas around the village of Bédar
Temperatures of approximately 40°C have fueled wildfires across southern Europe this summer, with major incidents also reported in France and Portugal

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Detailed account of British victims and described it as Andalusia's 'most devastating fire'

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Brief video report emphasizing bodies found in vehicles

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Headline framed within broader European heatwave context

top_stories.headline: Bayeux Tapestry Returns to Britain for First Time in Nearly 1,000 Years
bullets: The 70-metre medieval tapestry arrived at the British Museum in London early Friday following a secret 11-hour journey through the Channel Tunnel
The tapestry was transported folded accordion-style in a climate-controlled case inside a shock-absorbing cradle, escorted by police
It will go on display from September 10, 2026 until July 2027, marking its first time on English soil since it was likely created there after 1066
The loan is seen as symbolic of improving French-British relations following Brexit

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Emphasized historic significance and provided detailed logistics of the journey

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Focused on the high-security overnight operation

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Used 'smuggled' in headline, emphasized post-Brexit diplomatic context

top_stories.headline: SK Hynix Raises $26.5 Billion in Largest-Ever US Listing by Foreign Firm
bullets: South Korean memory chip maker SK Hynix sold 177.9 million American depositary shares at $149 each on the Nasdaq
The company's market value topped $1 trillion in South Korea in May, with shares more than tripling this year amid AI chip demand
SK Hynix is a key supplier to Nvidia and one of the world's leading memory chip makers
The listing gives SK Hynix easier access to US investment capital with fewer barriers than South Korea

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized AI boom context and comparison to SpaceX's recent listing

additional_stories.headline: China successfully lands reusable rocket booster for the first time, challenging US dominance in space technology
France's climate watchdog warns country is unprepared for accelerating warming after temperatures rise 2.2°C since 1900
Ukraine and US reach political agreement on Patriot missile production licenses, Zelensky announces
Ukrainian intelligence agent reverses confession in murder of Monaco bombing suspect
Ukraine claims 25+ Russian ships hit in Sea of Azov over four days in 'logistics lockdown' campaign
Developing countries spent more on debt repayment than education in 2025, UNESCO reports
South Sudan marks 15 years of independence amid economic collapse and political deadlock
New Zealand considers joining Australia-Fiji Ocean of Peace Alliance amid Pacific security shifts
Palestinian aid worker killed in Israeli strike in Gaza mourned as one of territory's most recognized humanitarian figures
Australian unions announce strike at world's largest iron ore port, threatening BHP exports to Asia
